note: Austria adopted the euro at 13.7603 ATS per EUR. The schilling ceased to be legal tender after 28 February 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 1 March 2002.
Belgium adopted the euro at 40.3399 BEF per EUR. The franc ceased to be legal tender after 28 February 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 1 March 2002.
Bulgaria adopted the euro at 1.95583 BGN per EUR. Council Decision of 8 July 2025.
Redenomination of the Belarusian ruble effective 1 July 2016 per Presidential Edict No. 450 of 4 November 2015.
Germany adopted the euro at 1.95583 DEM per EUR. The mark ceased to be legal tender on 31 December 2001; the euro became sole legal tender from 1 January 2002.
Estonia adopted the euro at 15.6466 EEK per EUR on 1 January 2011. Kroon ceased to be legal tender on 15 January 2011.
Spain adopted the euro at 166.386 ESP per EUR. The peseta ceased to be legal tender after 28 February 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 1 March 2002.
France adopted the euro at 6.55957 FRF per EUR. The franc ceased to be legal tender after 17 February 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 18 February 2002.
Croatia adopted the euro at 7.53450 HRK per EUR. Council Decision (EU) 2022/1211 of 12 July 2022.
Irish pound ceased to be legal tender after 9 February 2002. Euro became sole legal tender from 10 February 2002. Fixed at 0.787564 IEP per EUR.
Italy adopted the euro at 1936.27 ITL per EUR. The lira ceased to be legal tender after 28 February 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 1 March 2002.
The Netherlands adopted the euro at 2.20371 NLG per EUR. The guilder ceased to be legal tender after 27 January 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 28 January 2002.
Portugal adopted the euro at 200.482 PTE per EUR. The escudo ceased to be legal tender after 28 February 2002; the euro became sole legal tender from 1 March 2002.
Bank of Sierra Leone redenominated the leone on 1 July 2022 at 1000 SLL = 1 SLE. Old leone remained legal tender during transition until 1 January 2024.
Banco Central de São Tomé e Príncipe redenominated the dobra on 1 January 2018 at 1000 STD = 1 STN.
BCV redenominated to bolívar soberano on 20 August 2018 at 100000 VEF = 1 VES.
Bank of Zambia rebased the kwacha on 1 January 2013 at 1000 ZMK = 1 ZMW. Old kwacha remained legal tender until 30 June 2013.
